title: Orphaned CI Detection
dataSources: ServiceNow configuration items (snow:cmdb_ci) with owner, support group, and business service mapping
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" operational_status="operational"
| where isnull(assigned_to) OR isnull(support_group) OR isnull(u_service)
| table name, ci_class, assigned_to, support_group, u_service
description: CIs without owners or service mappings aren't managed during incidents, creating accountability gaps and shadow infrastructure.
value: CIs without owners or service mappings aren't managed during incidents, creating accountability gaps and shadow infrastructure.
implementation: Query CMDB for operational CIs missing key attributes (owner, support group, service mapping). Report on orphaned CI inventory. Assign ownership through automated or manual workflow. Track orphan reduction over time.
visualization: Table (orphaned CIs), Pie chart (by CI class), Bar chart (orphans by missing attribute), Single value (total orphaned CIs).
knownFalsePositives: Coverage gaps while onboarding new systems, after mergers, during discovery or field changes, or when teams skip owner updates in busy months.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ServiceNow configuration items (snow:cmdb_ci) with owner, support group, and business service mapping.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Query CMDB for operational CIs missing key attributes (owner, support group, service mapping). Report on orphaned CI inventory. Assign ownership through automated or manual workflow. Track orphan reduction over time.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" operational_status="operational"
| where isnull(assigned_to) OR isnull(support_group) OR isnull(u_service)
| table name, ci_class, assigned_to, support_group, u_service
```

Understanding this SPL

**Orphaned CI Detection** — CIs without owners or service mappings aren't managed during incidents, creating accountability gaps and shadow infrastructure.

Documented **Data sources**: CMDB CI attributes. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(assigned_to) OR isnull(support_group) OR isnull(u_service)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Orphaned CI Detection**): table name, ci_class, assigned_to, support_group, u_service


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow configuration item or related CMDB view. Spot-check a few CIs, owners, and relationship fields, then confirm discovery dates if we measure freshness.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (orphaned CIs), Pie chart (by CI class), Bar chart (orphans by missing attribute), Single value (total orphaned CIs).
grandmaExplanation: We find things that have no good parent in the book of record, so ownership and support lines stay clear.
